evals.prompt: Precisamos de documentar o processo de intake de novos clientes da WizardingCode para poder delegá-lo. É o processo que mais tempo me consome — ~3h por cliente.
evals.expected_output: A process inventory classification (Estratégico×Delegável quadrant) for the client intake process, a complete POP document with trigger/inputs/outputs/SLA/tools/numbered steps/quality criteria/escalation, and a delegation handoff card.
expectations: The skill calls kb_search and kb_ask for the WizardingCode vault POP templates (specifically the lead intake and client onboarding D14 templates), the Dan Martell No-How-To Delegation framework, and any existing POPs in the KB before generating.

The Estratégico×Delegável classification is applied to the intake process first, producing the quadrant placement and the correct action — the intake process (rule-based, template-driven) should land in Operacional+Ready ('Delegate now').

The POP document is produced in the exact required structure: POP ID (POP-OPS-NNN), Owner (Role not name), Version 1.0, Created date, Trigger, Inputs, Outputs, SLA, Tools, numbered steps with action-first verbs, quality criteria (measurable not subjective), common errors, and escalation.
Each step uses action-first verbs ('Confirm', 'Send', 'Create', 'Record') — passive steps or vague steps ('ensure quality') are absent.

The delegation handoff card is produced with the three-repetition rule (Shadow × 1 → Pair × 2 → Supervised × 3 → Autonomous), the autonomous-by date, and a review cadence — and the POP is ingested via kb_ingest.

evals.prompt: Documenta o processo de fecho mensal financeiro — reconciliação de contas, IVA, e relatório para o contabilista. Este processo é do fundador ou pode ser delegado?
evals.expected_output: A classification of the monthly financial close as Estratégico+Ready or Operacional+Ready (depending on whether sign-off is required), a complete POP document, and a delegation handoff card targeting a finance assistant or AI agent.
expectations: The skill calls kb_search for the WizardingCode vault 'monthly close' POP template and any existing financial process documentation before generating.

The classification is applied: the monthly close involves statutory obligations (IVA, accountant liaison) which may require founder sign-off → Estratégico+Ready (delegate execution, founder reviews output) — this quadrant classification is explicitly stated with its action.
The POP includes the decision point for IVA submission: 'IF IVA discrepancy > €X → escalate to founder within 24h; ELSE → submit as normal' — the branching logic is explicit, not implicit.
Quality criteria include measurable compliance signals: reconciliation balanced to €0 variance, IVA submitted before statutory deadline, accountant report sent within SLA.

The handoff card identifies whether delegation target is a human finance assistant or an AI agent (AI agent is viable for reconciliation data entry; human for IVA statutory submission) — not a generic 'delegate to someone'.

evals.prompt: Build me a POP for our weekly LinkedIn content publishing process. We post 3 pieces per week — one thought leadership article, one client case study snippet, and one poll or question.
evals.expected_output: A complete POP for the weekly LinkedIn publishing workflow with the Estratégico×Delegável classification, numbered steps for all three content types, quality criteria per content type, and a delegation handoff card.
expectations: The skill calls kb_search for any prior content POPs or marketing process documentation in the KB, and the company profile for the content strategy context, before generating.

The process inventory step is run first: the weekly LinkedIn publishing workflow is classified on both axes (Strategic: the thought leadership article may require founder voice → Estratégico+Ready; the poll/question is Operacional+Ready).
The POP document covers all three content types with numbered steps that distinguish between them — it is not a generic 'post content' step.

Quality criteria per content type are measurable: thought leadership (≥ 500 words, ≥ 1 data point or case reference, reviewed by founder before publish), case study snippet (client approval received, results specific and quantified), poll (question is open and unbiased).
The POP is ingested via kb_ingest with the correct title format 'POP — LinkedIn Content Publishing — WizardingCode — v1.0' and tags: pop, sop, processo, marketing, wizardingcode.
